title: Prosthechea ionocentra
taxonomy: Prosthechea ionocentra is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Cymbidieae. The genus Prosthechea is a part of the subtribe Cymbidiinae. Prosthechea ionocentra is native to Central America. This orchid species is also known as the ionocentre orchid.

intro: Prosthechea ionocentra, also known as Ion's Prosthechea, is an epiphytic orchid species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help in reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Prosthechea ionocentra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Prosthechea ionocentra is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory infections and other inflammatory conditions.
